# Plan - 2026-06-24

Source brief: `.github/research/2026-06-24-research.md`.

The 2026-06-24 brief surfaced exactly three actionable items (one
correctness fix in MCP dispatch, one TUI responsiveness fix, one
docs lock-in). We create three issues, not five: padding the
backlog with speculative work would violate the "every issue must
cite a research source" rule. Open `auto-implement` queue before
this run: 0.

## Plan

### Item #1 - MCP tool-name namespacing (mcp, small ~3h)

**Files**

- Modify `src/mcp/client.ts` (extend `McpToolInfo`, add
  `mapToolInfo` qualified-name composition, add
  `escapeServerName` / `parseQualifiedName` /
  `getToolByQualifiedName` helpers, leave existing
  `callTool(serverName, toolName, args)` signature alone).
- Add `tests/mcp/qualified-name.test.ts` covering:
  collision of two mock servers exposing `read`, `:` in
  server name round-trip, `::` inside tool name surviving
  the split-on-last-`::`, `%` in server name escape
  ordering.

**Approach**

- Escape order: `%` first, then `:` -> `%3A`. Reverse in
  the opposite order.
- Qualified shape: `${escapedServer}::${tool}`.
- Keep raw `name` and `serverName` on `McpToolInfo` so the
  existing `cli/tui/dialogs/McpManager.tsx` group-by-server
  UI keeps working unchanged.
- No migration of `src/cli/interactive.ts` in this issue;
  it continues to use `callTool(serverName, toolName,
  args)`. A follow-up issue can switch it to qualified
  names once the agent loop wires MCP tools in.

**Risks**

- Double-escape if `:` is processed before `%`. Test
  matrix covers this explicitly.
- A consumer that today indexes `getAllTools()` results by
  `name` (string equality) will now see duplicate `name`
  entries from colliding servers. Search confirmed only
  `client.ts` itself and `cli/interactive.ts` consume
  `getAllTools()`, and neither indexes by `name`.

### Item #2 - FilePicker walk: abort, yield, throttle (tool, small ~3h)

**Files**

- New `src/cli/tui/dialogs/filePicker-domain.ts` (extract
  `listFiles` with `AbortSignal`, `setImmediate` yield every
  50 entries, optional `onBatch` callback).
- Modify `src/cli/tui/dialogs/FilePicker.tsx` (wire
  `AbortController` into the mount `useEffect`, abort on
  unmount and on `rootDir` prop change, 200ms debounce on
  `setAllFiles`).
- New `tests/cli/tui/filePicker-domain.test.ts` (happy path
  with 120-file temp dir, pre-aborted signal rejects with
  `AbortError`, depth cap respected). Temp dirs via
  `fs.mkdtemp` / teardown in `afterEach`.
- Extend `tests/cli/tui/FilePicker.test.tsx` with mount /
  immediate-unmount test asserting no `setState`-after-
  unmount warning (spy `console.error`).

**Approach**

- Yielding via `await new Promise<void>((r) =>
  setImmediate(r))` every 50 entries lets Ink reconcile.
- Abort check before each recursive call AND at the top of
  the per-entry loop, so deep trees abort promptly.
- Debounce protects against fast `rootDir` prop churn
  (typing into a `cd` input, for example).

**Risks**

- Tests for Ink components run on Node (not jsdom);
  `act` is not imported. The existing
  `tests/cli/tui/FilePicker.test.tsx` uses
  `ink-testing-library` `render` / `lastFrame`, no `act`
  needed. Stick to that pattern.

### Item #3 - Document MCP tool-key composition (config, small ~2h)

**Files**

- Modify `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` only (new `### MCP tool key
  composition` subsection under existing `## MCP
  Integration` at line 796; one-line forward link from the
  overview).

**Approach**

- Code-block examples for both escape directions.
- Spell out display-vs-routing rule (UI may strip prefix;
  permissions / hooks / routing must use the full
  qualified key).
- No source changes — purely docs lock-in for Item #1.

**Risks**

- None. `format:check` is the only CI gate that meaningfully
  applies.

## Sequencing

1. Item #1 (correctness) lands first.
2. Item #3 (docs) can be parallelized with or land with Item #1
   in the same PR window.
3. Item #2 (UX) is independent and parallelizable from day one.
